info.name: Telco Vulnerable GraphQL API
info.description:
Postman collection demonstrating OWASP API Security Top 10 vulnerabilities in the GraphQL implementation. Each request shows a specific security vulnerability for educational purposes.

**Setup:**
1. Start GraphQL server: `cd graphql && npm start`
2. Server runs on: `https://localhost:4000/graphql`
3. Disable SSL verification in Postman (Settings > General > SSL certificate verification OFF)

**Default Users:**
- Regular User 1: username / password
- Regular User 2: username2 / password  
- Admin User: admin / password

item.name: 1. Authentication

request.description: Login with default user credentials. Token is automatically saved to environment variable.
Login as admin user. Admin token saved separately.
Register a new regular user account.

🔴 VULNERABILITY: Mass Assignment

Registers a user with isAdmin=true. The API accepts this field without validation, allowing privilege escalation during registration.
Get the currently authenticated user's information.

item.description: Authentication mutations including login, registration, and mass assignment vulnerability demonstration.

item.name: 2. BOLA - Broken Object Level Authorization

request.description: Create an order for the authenticated user. Order ID is saved for later use.

🔴 VULNERABILITY: BOLA (Broken Object Level Authorization)

This request uses a regular user's token but queries another user's orders by specifying their customerId. The API does not validate that the customerId belongs to the authenticated user.

**Exploit:** Change customerId to any other user's ID to see their orders.

🔴 VULNERABILITY: BOLA Amplification via Batch Queries

GraphQL allows batching multiple queries in one request. This amplifies BOLA by accessing multiple users' data simultaneously.

🔴 VULNERABILITY: BOLA

Access any order by ID without ownership validation. Replace ANY_ORDER_ID_HERE with another user's order ID.

item.description: Demonstrates Broken Object Level Authorization vulnerabilities where users can access other users' data.

item.name: 3. Mass Assignment

request.description: Legitimate order update - changing quantity only.

🔴 VULNERABILITY: Mass Assignment

The mutation accepts any field from UpdateOrderInput without restrictions. Users can modify protected fields like 'status' which should only be changed by the system.

item.name: Mass Assignment - Modify Product Order State

request.description:
🔴 VULNERABILITY: Mass Assignment

Modify product order state, priority, and other protected fields without authorization.

item.description: Demonstrates mass assignment vulnerabilities where users can modify protected fields.

item.name: 4. BFLA - Broken Function Level Authorization

request.description:
🔴 VULNERABILITY: BFLA (Broken Function Level Authorization)

A regular user can delete any account including admin accounts. The mutation only checks if the user is authenticated, not if they have admin privileges or own the account being deleted.

🔴 VULNERABILITY: BFLA

Delete another regular user's account without authorization.

item.description: Demonstrates Broken Function Level Authorization where users can access admin functions.

request.description:
🔴 VULNERABILITY: SSRF (Server-Side Request Forgery)

The triggerWebhook mutation accepts any URL without validation. This can be exploited to:
- Access cloud metadata (AWS, Azure, GCP)
- Scan internal networks
- Access localhost services
- Exfiltrate data

🔴 VULNERABILITY: SSRF

Access internal MongoDB service on localhost. Can reveal service information.

🔴 VULNERABILITY: SSRF

Scan internal network addresses that are not accessible from outside.

🔴 VULNERABILITY: Introspection Enabled

GraphQL introspection is enabled, allowing attackers to enumerate the entire API schema, discover all types, queries, mutations, and their arguments. This reveals the complete attack surface.
List all available queries and their arguments via introspection.
Enumerate all mutations available in the API.

item.name: 7. Batch Query Attacks

request.description:
🔴 VULNERABILITY: No Rate Limiting on Batch Operations

GraphQL allows batching multiple mutations in a single request. Without rate limiting, attackers can create hundreds of records in one request.

🔴 VULNERABILITY: No Query Complexity Limits

Deeply nested query that fetches all fields. Without complexity limits, this can cause resource exhaustion.

item.description: Demonstrates GraphQL-specific batch query and complexity attacks.

request.description: Create a product order (TMF622-style).
Get product orders with optional filters.
